findings.title: Disabled failover still resets shared atomics on every URMA result
findings.impact: When urma_failover_success_rate_ratio is set to 0.0, the feature is documented as disabled, but every hot Set/Get URMA result still enters RecordUrmaResult and calls ResetWindow. That performs several release atomic stores on the per-worker tracker for every request, so a high-throughput client that disables failover still pays shared cache-line traffic and contention on the foreground data path.
findings.fix: Make the disabled path a true no-op after state has been cleared, for example by clearing only on the transition to disabled or by having callers skip RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult while the ratio is 0.0.
